pattern: Split the sentence into words and return the 1-based index of the first word that starts with the search word.
hints: Split the sentence on spaces to get the individual words, then iterate with their 1-based positions.
For each word, check whether it starts with searchWord using a string startsWith / prefix comparison.
Return the position of the first match, or -1 if none is found.

edge_inputs.description: single word that does not match
search word not a prefix of any word
two words match; must return the first
input: this problem is an easy problem

edge_inputs.description: searchWord equals exactly one of the words

stress.description: sentence of 10 words with searchWord always matching one
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import string
    words = [''.join(rng.choice(string.ascii_lowercase) for _ in range(rng.randint(2, 8))) for _ in range(size)]
    idx = rng.randint(0, size - 1)
    prefix_len = rng.randint(1, len(words[idx]))
    search = words[idx][:prefix_len]
    return [' '.join(words), search]
